zh.lifeSummary: 當William Kniveton of Ashbourne於1275年出生在英國德比夏爾梅爾卡斯頓時，他的父親Sir Matthew III Kniveton27歲，母親Matilda Stretton26歲。他於1301年之前與Margery在英國德比夏爾梅爾卡斯頓結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。他去世於1334年之後在英國德比夏爾阿希布恩，埋葬在英國德比夏爾布拉德利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Kniveton of Ashbourne于1275年出生在英国德比夏爾梅爾卡斯頓时，他的父亲Sir Matthew III Kniveton27岁，母亲Matilda Stretton26岁。他于1301年之前与Margery在英国德比夏爾梅爾卡斯頓结婚。他们至少是6个儿子的父母。他去世于1334年之后在英国德比夏爾阿希布恩，埋葬在英国德比夏爾布拉德利。

nameMeanings.name: Kniveton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Kniveton (Derbys) or Kneeton (Notts).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Ashbourne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Ashburn
meanings: English: habitational name from Ashbourne in Derbyshire, named from Old English æsc ‘ash tree’ + burna ‘stream’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When William Kniveton of Ashbourne was born in 1275, in Mercaston, Derbyshire, England, his father, Sir Matthew III Kniveton, was 27 and his mother, Matilda Stretton, was 26. He married Margery before 1301, in Mercaston, Derbyshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. He died after 1334, in Ashbourne, Derbyshire, England, and was buried in Bradley, Derbyshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Kniveton of Ashbourne est né en 1275, à Mercaston, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Matthew III Kniveton, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Matilda Stretton, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Margery avant 1301, à Mercaston, Derbyshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Il est décédé après 1334, à Ashbourne, Derbyshire, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Bradley, Derbyshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Kniveton of Ashbourne 1275 in Mercaston, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Matthew III Kniveton 27 und seine Mutter Matilda Stretton 26. Er hat vor 1301 in Mercaston, Derbyshire, England Margery geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne. Er starb nach 1334 in Ashbourne, Derbyshire, England und wurde in Bradley, Derbyshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Kniveton of Ashbourne è nato il nel 1275 nel Mercaston, Derbyshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Matthew III Kniveton, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Matilda Stretton, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Margery prima del 1301, nel Mercaston, Derbyshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli. Morì dopo il 1334, nel Ashbourne, Derbyshire, England, e fu sepolto a Bradley, Derbyshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William Kniveton of Ashbourneが1275年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアメルカストンで生まれた時，父Sir Matthew III Knivetonは27歳，母Matilda Strettonは26歳であった。1301年以前にイギリスデルブイシュアイアメルカストンでMargeryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子の親であった。彼は1334年以後にイギリスデルブイシュアイアアシュボウルンで死去し，イギリスデルブイシュアイアブラドリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Kniveton of Ashbourne(이)가 1275년 영국 더비실르 머카스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Matthew III Kniveton(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Stretton(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1301년 이전 영국 더비실르 머카스턴에서 Margery(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1334년 이후 영국 더비실르 애스h보우른에서 사망했으며 영국 더비실르 브래들리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Kniveton of Ashbourne nasceu em 1275, em Mercaston, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Matthew III Kniveton, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Matilda Stretton, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Margery antes 1301, em Mercaston, Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos. Ele faleceu após 1334, em Ashbourne, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Bradley, Derbyshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Kniveton of Ashbourne родился в 1275 г. в местности миркастон, дирбишир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Matthew III Kniveton было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda Stretton, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery перед 1301 г. в местности миркастон, дирбишир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Он умер после 1334 г. в местности ашбурн, дирбишир, Англия и был похоронен в местности брадли, дирбишир, Англия.
